What is a closure in JavaScript?
options: A function with no return value
A function that retains access to its outer scope even after the outer function returns
A method to close browser windows
A way to end a loop

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log(0.1 + 0.2 === 0.3)?
Which of the following is NOT a JavaScript data type?
What does Array.prototype.map() return?
options: A new array with results of calling a function on every element

questions.q: What is event delegation?
options: Attaching an event listener to a parent element to handle events from children

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log([] + [])?
Which keyword is used to create a block-scoped variable?
What does Promise.all() do?
options: Runs all promises in parallel and resolves when all resolve

questions.q: What is the purpose of the 'use strict' directive?
options: Enforce stricter parsing and error handling

questions.q: What is hoisting in JavaScript?
options: Moving code to the bottom of the file
Declarations being moved to the top of their scope during compilation

questions.q: Which method is used to convert a JSON string to a JavaScript object?

What does the 'this' keyword refer to inside an arrow function?
Which array method returns true if at least one element satisfies the condition?
What is the output of: console.log(2 ** 3)?
What does the optional chaining operator (?.) do?
options: Throws an error if property is null
Returns undefined instead of throwing an error when accessing null/undefined properties

questions.q: What is a generator function?
options: A function that can pause execution and resume later using yield
A function that generates random numbers

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log(typeof undefined)?
Which method can be used to flatten a nested array one level deep?
What is the difference between null and undefined?
options: null is assigned intentionally; undefined means a variable is declared but not assigned
undefined is an object; null is a string

questions.q: What does Array.from() do?
options: Creates a new array from array-like or iterable objects

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log(1 + '2')?

What does Object.freeze() do?
options: Makes the object immutable — no properties can be added, removed, or modified

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log([] == false)?
Which method can be used to find the index of an element in an array?
What is a Symbol in JavaScript?
options: A unique and immutable primitive value often used as object keys

questions.q: What does the nullish coalescing operator (??) return?
options: The left-hand value if it's falsy
The right-hand value if the left is null or undefined

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log(typeof function(){})?
Which method transforms each element of an array and flattens the result one level?
What is the event loop in JavaScript?
options: A loop that runs event handlers in parallel
A mechanism that handles asynchronous callbacks by processing the call stack and message queue

questions.q: What does the bind() method do?
options: Creates a new function with 'this' bound to the provided value

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log(!!'false')?
Which of the following creates a deep copy of an object?
What does async/await do?
options: Allows writing asynchronous code in a synchronous style using Promises

questions.q: What is prototype-based inheritance?
options: Objects inheriting directly from other objects via the prototype chain

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log(3 > 2 > 1)?
Which method returns a new array with elements that pass the test?
What is debouncing in JavaScript?
options: Delaying a function call until a specified time has passed since the last invocation

questions.q: What does Object.keys() return?
options: All own enumerable property names of an object as an array

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log(+'3')?
What is the purpose of WeakMap?
options: A map where keys are weakly referenced and can be garbage collected
A map for primitive types only

questions.q: What is memoization?
options: A way to store data in memory permanently
An optimization technique that caches function results based on inputs

questions.q: Which statement about const is correct?
options: const variables cannot be reassigned but object properties can be changed
const is the same as var
const allows reassignment in strict mode

questions.q: What is the output of: console.log(typeof class {})?
What does the reduce() method do?
options: Executes a reducer function on each element to produce a single output value
